mentions.note: 4.3% same-store sales growth; value retailer performing better than OLLI's 3.6% comp.
Worst S&P performer today but should be bought in downturns not sold; war-driven panic overdone; traditional recession proxy.
Expected to report Tuesday following Dollar Tree's success; value retailer likely to show continued resilience in consumer spending.

Core customers financially constrained; fuel costs rising, SNAP benefits reduced, trip consolidation lowering shopping frequency. Thesis for weak-economy play has reversed; no longer benefiting from economic stress.
Part of the unexplained retail buying frenzy; Cramer calls it a good one.
Value-oriented discount retailer; tangible product focus; attracting rotation from software.
Operational turnaround confirmed by direct store visits; management changes working; remains terrific despite 98% annual run.
Discount retailer with real bargains; reports Thursday; fits working category of discount plays as long as crude doesn't go insane; should go higher.
Trade-down retail theme; reports tomorrow; Cramer has blessing to buy; financially challenged families using discount channels.
Off game right now but historically pulls bull from hat; wouldn't bet against but proceed carefully.
Down 2.6%; should have turned positive as consumers trade down; indicating consumer hurt more than expected.
